ai_strategy_lead.title: Syracuse AI access and iSchool AI programs

documents.title: Claude for Education campuswide access announcement

ai_strategy.summary: Syracuse's strongest public AI signals are campuswide Claude for Education access through Anthropic, a new Applied Human-Centered AI master's program, open-source GenAI development guidance, and AI research/funding coordination. The posture is practical, human-centered, and enterprise-access oriented.

key_provisions: Use AI in accordance with academic integrity expectations and instructor or unit guidance
Do not enter restricted, confidential, regulated, or sensitive data into unapproved AI tools
Review AI outputs for accuracy, hallucination, bias, copyright, and attribution issues
Disclose or cite AI use when required by course, research, publisher, or unit rules
Keep human judgment and accountability in consequential academic, research, and administrative decisions
ai_policy.summary: Syracuse University's AI policy posture is best characterized as comprehensive: public sources show explicit expectations for responsible use, privacy, disclosure, academic integrity, and human review, while leaving some implementation decisions to units or instructors.

governance_structure.summary: Syracuse University's public AI governance is organized through Office of Academic Affairs / Provost, Syracuse Open Source Program Office.

initiatives.title: Applied Human-Centered AI master's program
initiatives.description: Syracuse iSchool's M.S. in Applied Human-Centered AI is a 31-credit program focused on human-AI interaction, responsible AI, NLP, building GenAI applications, deep learning, and portfolio work.

narrative_summary: Syracuse University (Syracuse, NY) is an R1 institution whose public AI posture shows activity across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tooling, workforce readiness, and access. Syracuse's strongest public AI signals are campuswide Claude for Education access through Anthropic, a new Applied Human-Centered AI master's program, open-source GenAI development guidance, and AI research/funding coordination. The posture is practical, human-centered, and enterprise-access oriented. The most concrete public initiatives captured here are Campuswide Claude for Education partnership; Applied Human-Centered AI master's program; Responsible AI and human-AI curriculum; Syracuse OSPO GenAI guidance; AI research funding coordination. Across the profile, the strongest evidence comes from official university AI hubs, policy pages, teaching resources, research-center pages, and program pages rather than secondary reporting. The institution_type is set to r1_research from the canonical Carnegie R1 list. Known gaps are intentionally explicit: leadership names, user counts, detailed budgets, and compute counts are only included when they surfaced in public sources. Overall, the profile supports at least five AI initiatives and evidences strategy/leadership, governance, teaching/curriculum, research infrastructure, student services or enterprise tooling, workforce/community, partnerships, and equity dimensions where public sources permit.
